verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein residing primarily in the ER membrane, where its luminal EF-hand domain senses Ca2+ store depletion. Upon store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctions, where its cytoplasmic SOAR/CAD domain directly gates Orai channels at the plasma membrane. Crucially, a fraction of STIM1 is also present at the plasma membrane itself with its N-terminal domain facing the extracellular space, making it directly accessible from the extracellular face. Anti-STIM1 antibodies targeting the extracellular domain block SOCE, confirming surface accessibility.
